findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The Mapping Strategy hierarchical diagram (showing boxes for Site Usage, Building Usage, Process Usage, Other Usage, M/U with directional arrows from External and Internal Data sources) is not preserved as a visual element. It is decomposed into nested text bullet lists, losing the diagram and its visual flow relationships.
findings.evidence: PDF page 10 shows a structured box diagram with dark blue boxes arranged hierarchically (Site Usage → Building Usage → Process Usage / Other Usage → M / U) and two arrows indicating External Data flows down and Internal Data flows up into the structure. The HTML renders this only as nested bullet lists (Building Usage > Process Usage > M, U; Other Usage > M, U repeated three times) with no diagram image, unlike other complex diagrams in the presentation (pages 12–13) which are preserved as images.

findings.description: The 'Note on clean steam setup' paragraph at the bottom of the Natural Gas and Steam diagram (PDF page 12) is missing from the HTML, even though other text elements from the same diagram (legend items, equipment labels, general notes) were extracted as HTML text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 12 includes: 'Note on clean steam setup: For each full generator's 125A and 125B can supply both of parallel M streams. Currently, each still generator is set to low pressure as a backup to high pressure generators on C5 supply…The generators set to low pressure varies depending on the day.' This paragraph does not appear anywhere in the HTML.
